codeReview.summary: The fix adequately addresses all three issues from the prior review (2 blocking, 1 non-blocking). (1) CSS definitions for all oauth-* classes used in oauth_status.html have been added to the settings.html <style> block — 29 CSS class selectors covering card layout, status dots, buttons, text colors, meta display, and empty state. The CSS uses existing CSS custom properties (--green, --red, --amber, --surface-2, --border, etc.) ensuring correct dark mode colors. The body.light selector overrides these variables for light mode, so all OAuth classes automatically adapt. (2) HTMX library include (<script src="/ui/static/htmx.min.js"></script>) has been added to settings.html in the <head>, matching the pattern used by all other pages (dashboard, config, models). The 30-second auto-refresh via hx-trigger="every 30s" will now function. (3) A 'Check Status' button using HTMX (hx-get, hx-target, hx-swap) has been added for Copilot backends, providing manual status refresh capability. 5 new tests verify all three fixes. One minor observation: .oauth-status-dot-missing CSS is not explicitly defined — the dot would show as transparent/invisible for unauthenticated backends, which is acceptable but could benefit from an explicit gray style for completeness.

issues.description: The .oauth-status-dot-missing CSS class is not defined. When TokenState="missing" (unauthenticated), the status dot renders with only the base .oauth-status-dot styles (10px round) but no background color — it will be invisible. A reasonable default would be background: var(--text-dim) or a gray color to provide a visual 'not connected' indicator. This is non-blocking because the missing state also shows 'Not connected' text next to the dot.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The prior review noted that settings.html was the only main page missing the HTMX include. This has been fixed. A useful convention to document would be: 'All pages that include HTMX-powered fragments or use hx-* attributes must include the HTMX script tag in their <head>'. This could prevent similar omissions in future template additions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: settings.html now includes HTMX at line 784, matching dashboard.html, config.html, and models.html. The prior review's sharedStateObservation[0] flagged this gap.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The pattern for adding CSS for template fragments is: add CSS class definitions to the inline <style> block of the page that renders the fragment, using existing CSS custom properties from :root/body.light for theming. This is a useful pattern to document for future workers adding visual components.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The fix adds ~160 lines of CSS to settings.html's <style> block, using var(--green), var(--red), var(--amber), var(--surface-2), var(--border) etc. from :root (dark) and body.light (light) variable definitions.

summary: The fix successfully addresses all three issues from the prior scrutiny review. (1) All 29 oauth-* CSS classes used in oauth_status.html now have definitions in settings.html's inline style block, using CSS custom properties that support both dark and light modes. (2) HTMX library include has been added to settings.html, enabling the 30-second auto-refresh. (3) A 'Check Status' button for Copilot backends uses HTMX for immediate status refresh. 5 new tests cover all fixes. One minor observation: .oauth-status-dot-missing has no explicit background color, leaving the dot invisible for unauthenticated backends, but this is non-blocking since 'Not connected' text is shown alongside. Overall, the fix is clean, well-tested, and follows existing codebase patterns.
